prompt.customInstructions: eBay search: GET https://www.ebay.com/sch/i.html?_nkw=<query>. Server-rendered HTML; no JS/auth needed. Each result is a <li class="s-card"> inside <ul class="srp-results">. The response contains promo/placement cards with title "Shop on eBay" that MUST be filtered out. An .srp-save-null-search banner signals "No exact matches found" and any remaining cards are suggestions, not matches — return []. Card titles embed a hidden a11y span "Opens in a new window or tab" that should be stripped. Anchor hrefs are wrapped in tracking redirects; reconstruct a clean product URL from the <li data-listingid="..."> attribute as https://www.ebay.com/itm/<listingId>.
prompt.knownIssues: Bot wall: a plain Node/undici fetch without a real-browser User-Agent returns the "Pardon Our Interruption" splash (~13 KB, zero s-card elements). Always send a desktop-Chrome UA header (e.g. Mozilla/5.0 ... Chrome/131 Safari/537.36). Currency depends on visitor geolocation/cookie (USD by default for a cookieless US fetch); parse the rendered symbol (US $, AU $, C $, €, £, ¥, …) and map to ISO 4217. Prices can be ranges like "$10.00 to $20.00" — take the first numeric amount. Brand is not reliably extractable from the title; default to "unknown" and let downstream normalizers infer. The first 1-2 cards on most result pages are sponsored placements whose title is literally "Shop on eBay"; filter them by text, not by position.
prompt.apiNotes: eBay has a real Browse API (api.ebay.com/buy/browse/v1) but it requires an OAuth client_credentials token. The scraped SRP endpoint is sufficient for price/title/image and matches the price shown on ebay.com; keep using GET /sch/i.html?_nkw= unless API credentials are wired into the adapter (meta.needsAuth=true).
